seo.title: California Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to California - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity california, how to transfer dietitian license to california, dietitian compact california, california dietitian endorsement application

board.name: No State Licensing Board
board.parent_dept: Title Protection Only (Business and Professions Code Section 2585)
board.mailing_address: 120 South Riverside Plaza, Suite 2000, Chicago, IL 60606-6995

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: No state fees (CDR national fees apply)

license_types.title: Registered Dietitian (RD) / Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN)
license_types.description: California does not license dietitians. However, under state law, you cannot use the title 'Registered Dietitian' unless you hold current, active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Complete Academic Requirements
steps.description: Successfully complete a minimum of a bachelor's degree (a graduate degree is required as of January 1, 2024) and all required coursework accredited by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND®) at a U.S. regionally accredited university or college.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Practice Program
steps.description: Successfully complete an ACEND®-accredited supervised practice program, typically referred to as a dietetic internship.

steps.title: Apply for and Pass the RDN Examination
steps.description: Apply for the Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) examination through the MyCDR portal once your education and supervised practice are verified. After receiving authorization, schedule and pass the RDN examination.

steps.title: Maintain RDN Credential
steps.description: Maintain your RDN credential through the MyCDR portal by fulfilling continuing professional education requirements and paying the annual registration maintenance fee.

documents.detail: Employers will require proof of your active CDR credential.

provisional_text: Because there is no state licensing board, there are no state-issued provisional permits in California.
reciprocity_text: If you are a licensed dietitian moving to California from another state, you do not need to transfer your license. As long as your national CDR registration is active, you are legally permitted to practice.

faqs.question: How much does a California dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: California does not charge a state licensing fee because the state does not issue dietitian licenses. You only pay your standard national registration and exam fees directly to the CDR.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for a California Dietitian?
faqs.answer: Because there is no state board, there are no state-mandated CE hours. You simply follow the national CDR requirement of completing 75 CPEUs every five years to maintain your credential.

faqs.question: Does California have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: California is currently a CDR State. There is no separate state endorsement transfer pathway right now; maintain an active CDR credential to practice.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to California?
faqs.answer: This is currently a CDR State, so there is no state transfer timeline. You can usually start once your CDR credential and employer onboarding are complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in California while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: There is no active state endorsement application to wait on right now. Start dates are typically driven by employer credentialing and onboarding requirements.

reciprocity.processing_time: 1 to 2 Weeks (for exam authorization after application submission)
reciprocity.notes: If you are a licensed dietitian moving to California from another state, you do not need to transfer your license. As long as your national CDR registration is active, you are legally permitted to practice.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in California
hero.dek: California does not issue a state license for Dietitians or Nutritionists. To practice as a Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) in California, you must obtain and maintain the national RDN credential from the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) via their MyCDR portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Create an account on the MyCDR portal by selecting 'Register for an account' on the login page. You will then provide personal details and link your ACEND-accredited education and supervised practice experience to apply for the Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) examination.
